prompt:
Sprint work for the support-desk notebook (desk_notes.rb — the little tagged note store the agents live in during tickets). It does add/find/list/tag queries today and that all has to keep working exactly as is. Three features made the cut this sprint; the acceptance tests for all of them are already in test_desk_notes.rb next to the regression tests, so that file is the contract — don't edit it.

1) Archiving. Agents want stale macros out of the way without deleting history. `archive(id)` / `unarchive(id)`, and `archived?(id)`. Archived notes disappear from the default reads — `notes`, `tagged`, `tags`, and the new `search` — but every one of those takes `include_archived: true` to see everything. `find` by id always works, archived or not (links in old tickets must not rot). Any of archive/unarchive/archived? on an id we don't have raises ArgumentError with the message `no such note: 99` (whatever the id was).

2) Tag rename WITH merge. Tag sets drifted — we have 'billing' and 'escalated' meaning the same thing. `rename_tag(old, new)` retags every note carrying old (archived ones too), returns how many notes actually changed, and — the important part — renaming onto a tag that already exists MERGES: a note that carried both ends up with the tag once, tag lists stay sorted and duplicate-free like add() already guarantees. Renaming a tag no note carries raises ArgumentError with the message `unknown tag "zzz"` (old name, quoted). Renaming a tag onto itself is a no-op and returns 0.

3) Fuzzy title search, launcher-style. `search(pattern, include_archived: false)` returns notes whose TITLE contains the pattern's characters in order, not necessarily adjacent, case-insensitively — 'prin' should hit 'Printer jam checklist'. Ranking is pinned, so implement exactly this: match greedily left to right (each pattern character takes its first occurrence after the previous match); score the match by span = last matched index - first matched index + 1; order results by smaller span, then smaller first-match index, then note id. Empty pattern = no filter: every visible note in id order. Non-matching notes just don't appear.

Done when `ruby test_desk_notes.rb` is green top to bottom — the old regression tests and the new feature blocks.
